Velox
  • API Reference
  • View page source

API Reference

This section documents the core Velox API.

Velox Core

Core - Velox Framework

Modos de execucao:

  1. WSGI/Threading -> app.run() (sem deps, stdlib Python)

  2. ASGI/uvicorn -> app.run(asgi=True) (requer: pip install uvicorn)

    -> uvicorn meuapp:app (CLI do uvicorn diretamente)

Rotas sync e async funcionam juntas no mesmo app:

@app.get(‘/’) def home(req, res): # sync - igual ao antes

return app.render(‘index.html’)

@app.get(‘/api/dados’) async def dados(req, res): # async - roda no event loop

resultado = await buscar() res.json(resultado)

@app.websocket(‘/ws/chat’) async def chat(ws): # WebSocket (apenas ASGI)

while True:

msg = await ws.receive() if msg is None: break await ws.send(f’echo: {msg}’)

velox.core.Blueprint

alias of Router

velox.core.MeuFramework

alias of Velox

class velox.core.RoutePattern(path: str)[source]

Bases: object

__init__(path: str)[source]
match(path: str) → Dict | None[source]
class velox.core.Router(prefix: str = '')[source]

Bases: object

Router modular — use como Blueprint para separar rotas:

api = Router()

@api.get(‘/users’) async def list_users(req, res):

res.json(await User.all())

app.include(api, prefix=’/api’)

__init__(prefix: str = '')[source]
add_error_handler(code: int, handler: Callable)[source]
add_route(path: str, method: str, handler: Callable)[source]
delete(path: str)[source]
get(path: str)[source]
get_error_handler(code: int) → Callable | None[source]
list_routes() → List[Dict][source]
match(path: str, method: str) → Tuple[Callable | None, Dict][source]
patch(path: str)[source]
post(path: str)[source]
put(path: str)[source]
resource(path: str)[source]

Define múltiplos métodos HTTP para uma rota usando uma classe.

Uso:

@app.resource(‘/api/produtos’) class Produtos:

def get(req, res):

res.json({‘produtos’: []}) return res

def post(req, res):

body = req.json or {} res.json({‘criado’: True, ‘dados’: body}) return res

def put(req, res):

res.json({‘atualizado’: True}) return res

def delete(req, res):

res.json({‘deletado’: True}) return res

route(path: str, methods: List[str] = None)[source]
websocket(path: str)[source]
class velox.core.Velox(import_name: str = None)[source]

Bases: object

Velox - Fast Python Web Framework.

Modos:

app.run() -> WSGI threading (sem deps) app.run(asgi=True) -> ASGI uvicorn (pip install uvicorn) uvicorn meuapp:app -> ASGI diretamente pelo CLI

Exemplos rapidos:

app = Velox(__name__)

@app.get(‘/’) def home(req, res):

return app.render(‘index.html’, {‘nome’: ‘Mundo’})

@app.get(‘/api’) async def api(req, res):

data = await meu_servico() res.json(data)

@app.websocket(‘/ws’) async def ws(ws):

while True:

msg = await ws.receive() if not msg: break await ws.send(f’echo: {msg}’)

@app.on_startup async def init():

await db.connect()

app.run() # WSGI app.run(asgi=True) # ASGI

__init__(import_name: str = None)[source]
delete(path: str)[source]
error_handler(code: int)[source]
get(path: str)[source]
include(router: Router, prefix: str = '')[source]

Inclui um Blueprint/Router com prefixo

load_apps(app_names: List[str] = None)[source]

Carrega apps modulares (criados com velox startapp).

Uso:

app = Velox(__name__) app.load_apps([‘blog’, ‘api’, ‘usuarios’])

O sistema procura por:

  • {app}/views.py -> Registra routers/blueprints

  • {app}/admin.py -> Registra no painel admin

  • {app}/models.py -> Importa models

Parameters:

app_names – Lista de nomes de apps. Se None, autodiscover todos.

not_found(fn: Callable)[source]
on_shutdown(fn: Callable)[source]

Executado ao parar (apenas ASGI)

on_startup(fn: Callable)[source]

Executado ao iniciar (apenas ASGI)

patch(path: str)[source]
post(path: str)[source]
put(path: str)[source]
render(template_name: str, context: Dict = None) → str[source]
resource(path: str)[source]

Define múltiplos métodos HTTP para uma rota usando uma classe

route(path: str, methods: List[str] = None)[source]
routes() → List[Dict][source]

Lista todas as rotas registradas

run(host: str = None, port: int = None, debug: bool = None, asgi: bool = False, workers: int = 1, reload: bool = False)[source]

Inicia o servidor.

Parameters:

  • host – Host. Padrao: APP_HOST do .env ou ‘localhost’

  • port – Porta. Padrao: APP_PORT do .env ou 8000

  • debug – Modo debug. Padrao: APP_DEBUG do .env

  • asgi – True para uvicorn/ASGI (requer: pip install uvicorn) False para WSGI/threading (sem deps)

  • workers – Numero de workers ASGI (default: 1)

  • reload – Auto-reload em mudancas de arquivo (dev ASGI)

server_error(fn: Callable)[source]
static(folder: str = 'static')[source]
template(folder: str = 'templates')[source]
use(fn: Callable)[source]

Adiciona middleware global (sync ou async).

@app.use async def cors(next_handler, req, res, **kw):

res.set_header(‘Access-Control-Allow-Origin’, ‘*’) return await next_handler(req, res, **kw)

websocket(path: str)[source]

Rota WebSocket (apenas ASGI). Handler recebe ws: WebSocket

class velox.core.WebSocket(scope: Dict, receive: Callable, send: Callable)[source]

Bases: object

WebSocket para rotas ASGI.

Uso:

@app.websocket(‘/ws/chat’) async def chat(ws):

while True:

msg = await ws.receive() if msg is None: break await ws.send(f’echo: {msg}’)

# JSON helpers: data = await ws.receive_json() await ws.send_json({‘status’: ‘ok’})

__init__(scope: Dict, receive: Callable, send: Callable)[source]
async accept(subprotocol: str = None)[source]
async close(code: int = 1000)[source]
property closed: bool
async receive() → str | None[source]

Recebe mensagem de texto. Retorna None se conexao fechada.

async receive_bytes() → bytes | None[source]
async receive_json() → Any | None[source]
async send(data)[source]
async send_json(data: Any)[source]
velox.core.create_app() → Velox[source]

Request

Request - Velox Framework Representa uma requisicao HTTP recebida pelo servidor.

Propriedades disponiveis:

request.method -> ‘GET’, ‘POST’, etc request.path -> ‘/api/produtos’ request.ip -> IP real do cliente request.host -> host da requisicao request.headers -> dict de headers request.args -> query params (?page=1) request.form -> dados de formulario POST request.json -> body JSON request.data -> body raw bytes request.files -> arquivos enviados request.cookies -> cookies da requisicao request.is_ajax -> True se for requisicao XHR request.is_secure -> True se for HTTPS request.user_agent -> user agent do navegador request.referer -> pagina de origem request.content_type -> Content-Type do body request.content_length -> tamanho do body

class velox.request.Request(handler)[source]

Bases: object

Objeto de requisicao HTTP — disponivel em todo handler como primeiro parametro.

Uso:

@app.get(‘/api/info’) def info(request, response):

print(request.ip) # IP do cliente print(request.method) # GET print(request.path) # /api/info print(request.args) # query params print(request.form) # form POST print(request.json) # body JSON print(request.cookies) # cookies

__init__(handler)[source]
property accept_languages: list

[‘pt-BR’, ‘pt’, ‘en’]

Type:

Idiomas aceitos pelo cliente

property args: dict

Parametros da query string (?chave=valor).

Uso:

# URL: /buscar?q=notebook&page=2 q = request.args.get(‘q’) # ‘notebook’ page = request.args.get(‘page’, ‘1’) # ‘2’

property base_url: str

//meusite.com/api/produto’

Type:

URL sem query string

Type:

‘http

property content_length: int

Tamanho do body em bytes

property content_type: str

‘application/json’, ‘multipart/form-data’, etc

Type:

Content-Type do body

property cookies: dict

Cookies da requisicao como dict.

Uso:

token = request.cookies.get(‘token’) sessao = request.cookies.get(‘session_id’)

property data: bytes

Body raw da requisicao em bytes

property form: dict

Dados de formulario HTML (application/x-www-form-urlencoded).

Uso:

nome = request.form.get(‘nome’) email = request.form.get(‘email’)

property full_path: str

/api/produto?page=1

Type:

Caminho completo com query string

get(key: str, default=None)[source]

Busca valor em args ou form

property headers: dict

Headers da requisicao (case-insensitive)

property host: str

8000’

Type:

Host da requisicao

Type:

‘meusite.com’ ou ‘localhost

property ip: str

IP real do cliente. Funciona corretamente atras de nginx/proxy/load balancer.

Uso:

print(request.ip) # ‘177.92.10.45’

property is_ajax: bool

True se a requisicao foi feita via JavaScript (fetch/XHR).

Uso:
if request.is_ajax:

response.json({‘ok’: True})

else:

return app.render(‘pagina.html’)

property is_json: bool

True se o Content-Type for application/json

property is_secure: bool

True se a requisicao veio via HTTPS

property json

Body da requisicao como dict/list Python (application/json).

Uso:

dados = request.json nome = dados.get(‘nome’)

property language: str

‘pt-BR’

Type:

Primeiro idioma aceito pelo cliente

property method: str

GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH

Type:

Metodo HTTP

property path: str

/api/produto

Type:

Caminho da URL sem query string

property query_params: dict
property referer: str

URL da pagina de origem (Referer header)

property remote_addr: str

Alias para .ip — compatibilidade com Flask

property text: str

Body da requisicao como string UTF-8

property url: str

//meusite.com/api/produto?page=1’

Type:

URL completa

Type:

‘http

property user_agent: str

User-Agent do navegador/cliente

Response

Módulo Response - Manipulação de respostas HTTP

Type hints adicionados para melhorar IntelliSense e experiência do desenvolvedor.

class velox.response.Response(body: str | bytes | dict | list | None = '', status_code: int = 200, headers: Dict[str, str] | None = None)[source]

Bases: object

Classe que representa uma resposta HTTP

Fornece métodos fácil para retornar diferentes tipos de resposta: - HTML - JSON - Texto - Arquivos

Uso:

@app.get(‘/’) def home(req, res):

return res.html(‘<h1>Olá Mundo</h1>’)

@app.get(‘/api/dados’) def api(req, res):

return res.json({‘status’: ‘ok’, ‘data’: []})

__init__(body: str | bytes | dict | list | None = '', status_code: int = 200, headers: Dict[str, str] | None = None)[source]
body: str | bytes | dict | list | None
property content_type: str | None

Retorna o Content-Type da resposta

delete_cookie(name: str, path: str = '/') → Response[source]

Remove um cookie definindo expiração no passado

file(file_path: str, content_type: str | None = None) → Response[source]

Retorna um arquivo para download

property headers: Dict[str, str]

Retorna os headers da resposta

html(html_content: str, status_code: int = 200) → Response[source]

Retorna conteúdo HTML

json(data: Any, status_code: int = 200) → Response[source]

Retorna dados JSON

property location: str | None

Retorna o header Location (para redirects)

redirect(location: str, status_code: int = 302) → Response[source]

Redireciona para outra URL

send(content: Any, status_code: int = 200) → Response[source]

Envia conteúdo genérico como texto

set_cookie(name: str, value: str, expires: int | None = None, path: str = '/', secure: bool = False, httponly: bool = False, samesite: str | None = None) → Response[source]

Define um cookie na resposta.

Parameters:

  • name – Nome do cookie

  • value – Valor do cookie

  • expires – Tempo de expiração em segundos

  • path – Caminho do cookie

  • secure – HTTPS only

  • httponly – Inacessível via JavaScript

  • samesite – Política SameSite (‘Strict’, ‘Lax’, ‘None’)

set_header(name: str, value: str) → Response[source]

Define um header específico. Retorna self para chaining.

set_headers(headers: Dict[str, str]) → Response[source]

Define múltiplos headers de uma vez. Retorna self para chaining.

status_code: int
text(content: str, status_code: int = 200) → Response[source]

Retorna texto puro

Router / Blueprint

WebSocket

class velox.core.WebSocket(scope: Dict, receive: Callable, send: Callable)[source]

Bases: object

WebSocket para rotas ASGI.

Uso:

@app.websocket(‘/ws/chat’) async def chat(ws):

while True:

msg = await ws.receive() if msg is None: break await ws.send(f’echo: {msg}’)

# JSON helpers: data = await ws.receive_json() await ws.send_json({‘status’: ‘ok’})

__init__(scope: Dict, receive: Callable, send: Callable)[source]
async accept(subprotocol: str = None)[source]
async close(code: int = 1000)[source]
property closed: bool
async receive() → str | None[source]

Recebe mensagem de texto. Retorna None se conexao fechada.

async receive_bytes() → bytes | None[source]
async receive_json() → Any | None[source]
async send(data)[source]
async send_json(data: Any)[source]

Route Pattern

class velox.core.RoutePattern(path: str)[source]

Bases: object

__init__(path: str)[source]
match(path: str) → Dict | None[source]

Module Functions

velox.core.load_env(path='.env')

Loads environment variables from a .env file.

Parameters:

path (str) – Path to the .env file

Raises:

FileNotFoundError – If the file doesn’t exist

Example:

velox.core.load_env('.env.prod')

Constants

velox.core.CONVERTERS

Dictionary of URL parameter converters.

Type Aliases

velox.core.MeuFramework

Alias for velox.core.Velox (for backwards compatibility).

from velox import MeuFramework

is equivalent to:

from velox import Velox
velox.core.Blueprint

Alias for velox.core.Router.

from velox import Blueprint

is equivalent to:

from velox import Router
